---
title: Tools
description: The tools gaia and its souls can call, from the tool registry.
---

<!-- GENERATED by scripts/gen_reference.py — do not edit by hand. -->

Tools are what gaia and its souls call to act. On by default; gated by `tools.<id>.enabled`. The model invokes these — you don't.

| Tool | What it does |
|---|---|
| `ask_user` | Ask the user a question and wait for their reply before continuing. Use when |
| `cron` | Manage scheduled jobs: gaia runs a message later / on a recurring schedule |
| `exec` | Run a shell command in your workspace (install deps, build, test, CLIs). |
| `exec_kill` | Stop a background process you started with exec(..., background=True). |
| `exec_list` | List your background processes and whether each is still running. |
| `exec_poll` | Read a background process's output since the last poll (incremental); when |
| `fs_edit` | Replace text in a workspace file; refused unless the match count is exactly |
| `fs_glob` | Find workspace files matching a glob pattern. |
| `fs_grep` | Search file contents under the workspace. |
| `fs_read` | Read a UTF-8 text file from your workspace. |
| `fs_write` | Write a text file in your workspace. |
| `load_memory` | Loads the memory for the current user. |
| `remember` | Save a durable fact about the user to long-term memory (stable preferences |
| `serve` | Serve a built website locally so you can screenshot it or share a live preview. |
| `serve_list` | List the local sites you're currently serving (url, port, public_url, workspace). |
| `serve_stop` | Stop a local server started with serve (and its public tunnel, if any). |
| `task_complete` | Mark one of your tasks done and record its result + artifacts. |
| `task_create` | File a task on the board to track real work that should outlive this turn — a |
| `task_get` | Get one of your tasks by id (full detail). |
| `task_list` | List your tasks, optionally filtered by mission or status. |
| `task_update` | Update one of your tasks — change its status, append notes, or set an assignee. |
| `web_fetch` | Fetch a web page and return its readable content as markdown (nav/ads stripped). |
